records.summary: AureliaAppFrameMaterializer is the configuration frontier that materializes Aurelia facade, root container, AppRootConfig, and AppRoot products for a sequence.
records.rationale: The app frame is the bridge between recognized configuration flow and the spendable app/container/root products used by DI, controller, lifecycle, and rendering work. Keeping it explicit avoids pushing root-admission behavior into configuration recognition or app-world composition.
guidance: Start here when work touches Aurelia facade admission, root container products, app-root config, AppRoot identity, or AppRootUsesConfig claims.
Keep app-frame products distinct from per-step configuration products and app-world project phase orchestration.
Use configuration README and DI world-construction guidance before changing root container or app-root semantics.

records.summary: ContainerChildMaterializer is the DI frontier for runtime-created child containers, self resolvers, and context resolver slots at controller/container boundaries.
records.rationale: Recursive rendering, route component activation, and controller creation need child-container semantics without forcing those responsibilities into the broad DI world constructor or the Container class itself. This materializer is the hook for controller-local DI products.
guidance: Start here when controller, route, or rendering work creates child containers or context resolver slots.
Keep child-container publication separate from root/app frame creation and broad DI world construction.
Use framework.di and framework.rendering before changing resolver slot semantics at runtime-created boundaries.

records.summary: Container is large and intentionally close to the Aurelia DI concept it emulates.
records.rationale: The user explicitly prefers direct framework-concept naming over emulator suffix sprawl. Container size should be judged by framework fidelity and DI flow clarity, not by a generic small-class target.
guidance: Compare to framework.di before splitting Container.
If new DI behavior appears, decide whether it belongs in Container, world construction, or publication rather than adding compatibility shims.
Keep auLink mirroring and framework DI semantics visible when refactoring.

records.summary: DiWorldConstructor is the current broad DI world-construction pressure point.
records.rationale: This class is adjacent to evaluator-backed DI closure, registration publication, resolver effects, and product-record emission. It should be read before adding another local DI world or registration materialization path.
guidance: Check framework.di and framework.admission before extending this constructor from app pressure alone.

Framework registration effects can emit resolver slots, resource slots, app tasks, and root-shared factory slots; keep `registerFactory(...)` effects as `ContainerFactorySlot` products instead of modeling them as resolver shortcuts.
ValidationHtmlConfiguration uses this lane for the `IValidationController` factory while ValidationConfiguration/ValidationHtmlConfiguration also contribute validation service resolvers.
If repeated record construction appears, look for publication helpers before moving behavior into KernelStore.
Treat split decisions as framework-interface splits, not generic small-class cleanup.

anchors.summary: Registration spending work frame used during DI world construction.

records.summary: Resolver is the semantic-runtime DI resolver model and should remain close to the Aurelia framework resolver concept.
records.rationale: Resolver is auLink-backed and participates in container slots, registration spending, and framework resolver effects. Treat it as a semantic model rather than a generic data carrier or local publication detail.
guidance: Inspect Resolver before changing resolver slot products, registration strategies, or container lookup behavior.

Resolver slots should retain the modeled resolver object when DI publication owns it; do not fall back to resolver product handles when a framework-shaped method such as getFactory needs resolver state.

ResolverStrategy mirrors Aurelia's numeric resolver strategy enum; cached callback registration stores cached state on a callback resolver, and invalid strategy values claim invalid_resolver_strategy / AUR0005.

Resolver wrapper calls such as newInstanceOf(...) are source-recognized as resolver keys; invalid_new_instance_on_interface / AUR0017 is exact only when the container state is proven fresh and the wrapped interface has no default registration.
Use InstanceProvider for contextual provider semantics and no_instance_provided / AUR0013; keep it at provider-resolution level until a source/runtime lifecycle path proves a diagnostic.
Keep resolver model changes grounded in framework.di rather than local app pressure alone.
If resolver publication grows, route record emission through DI publication materializers instead of inflating Resolver.

anchors.summary: Semantic-runtime InstanceProvider model for contextual DI providers.

records.summary: DI world-publication materializers publish registries, resolvers, resource slots, self resolvers, app tasks, issue products, source evidence, claims, and open seams for DI world construction.
records.rationale: DI world construction has several distinct publication lanes. Keeping them grouped under one durable record makes future cleanup start from the common publication layer before moving behavior into Container or DiWorldConstructor.
guidance: Start here when work touches DI resolver, registry, resource slot, self resolver, app-task, issue, source, claim, or open-seam records.
Keep publication helpers in world-publication.ts unless a framework DI concept deserves its own direct semantic model.
If repeated KernelStoreRecord construction returns in DI, inspect these materializers before adding another local emitter.

anchors.summary: DI resolver and resolver-slot publication materializer.
Kernel record construction pressure in DI publication.

records.summary: DI owns source-backed container issue products for exact kernel diagnostics that match modeled DI execution contexts.
records.rationale: Aurelia's kernel has multiple DI diagnostic paths with different runtime phases. Source/static resource registration warns and skips when a local resource key already exists, while registerResolver can throw for resolver-backed resource-key insertion. Ambient resolve(...) throws no_active_container_for_resolve only when the framework current container is absent, and null_undefined_key only after an active container tries to resolve a nullish key. The inject helper throws invalid_inject_decorator_usage only for rejected TC39 decorator contexts. Semantic-runtime must keep those paths separate by execution context and decorator target kind.
guidance: Use DiIssues for source/static-resource duplicate key publication and cite kernel ErrorNames.resource_already_exists / AUR0007.
Keep the first modeled severity as warning because this is the framework register(...) warn/skip path, not the registerResolver(...) throw path.

Use DiResolveCallSite execution-context and active-container expectation before citing kernel ErrorNames.no_active_container_for_resolve / AUR0016; module/static evaluation can spend exact authority, instance activation and caller-dependent calls cannot.

Use DiResolveCallSite nullish key arguments before citing kernel ErrorNames.null_undefined_key / AUR0014; only container-activation contexts can spend that exact authority for ambient resolve because definitely-absent contexts throw AUR0016 first.

Use DiInjectDecoratorSite target-kind recognition before citing kernel ErrorNames.invalid_inject_decorator_usage / AUR0022; class and field targets are valid, method/getter/setter/accessor targets are exact invalid contexts under the TC39 decorator runtime, and legacy parameter decorators stay unclaimed until modeled separately.
Use DiDependencyCycleIssueMaterializer for source-visible singleton activation cycles reached from concrete container.get(...) entries before citing cyclic_dependency / AUR0003.
Use DiRegistrationSpendingCascade recursion detection before citing unable_auto_register / AUR0006; this is a registration-cascade guard, not a generic container API syntax issue.
Do not add more kernel framework-error constants until the DI, registration, resolver, or evaluator substrate owns the exact framework behavior.

anchors.summary: DI issue product publisher for kernel-backed resource and resolve-call diagnostics.
Exact kernel framework error-code labels used by DI.
Import-aware ambient resolve call recognition with execution context and active-container expectation.
Source-backed materializer for exact ambient resolve diagnostics.
Import-aware inject-family decorator recognition with exact target-kind classification.
Source-backed materializer for exact invalid inject decorator diagnostics.

records.summary: Container lookup/invoke diagnostics must flow through runtime-facing key shape, not syntax-only recognition.
records.rationale: Aurelia's Container branches on the runtime key value: direct invoke rejects native functions before container state, get/getResolver validate nullish keys before lookup, _jitRegister rejects non-functions and intrinsic constructors after resolver lookup misses, then reaches the configured default resolver, and getFactory can depend on an existing factory or resolver state. Semantic-runtime therefore needs ContainerLookupKey, resolver-object-backed slots, ContainerConfiguration default-resolver policy, and lookup failure states before claiming broad kernel errors such as AUR0002, AUR0009, or AUR0015.
guidance: Use container-key.ts and container-lookup.ts before adding DI diagnostics for container.get/getResolver/getFactory/invoke behavior.

Only cite no_construct_native_fn / AUR0015 for method-local paths such as container.invoke(Array) and container.getFactory(Array) where framework source proves container state cannot change the outcome.

Container lookup answers carry exact framework authority for failed JIT/factory/default-resolver branches once the emulator has performed a concrete lookup: none_resolver_found / AUR0002, unable_jit_non_constructor / AUR0009, no_jit_intrinsic_type / AUR0010, no_jit_interface / AUR0012, and no_construct_native_fn / AUR0015.

Container.getFactory must consult resolver state for non-function keys before failing; if there is no modeled resolver object behind a resolver slot, treat that as substrate pressure rather than inventing a syntax-only diagnostic.
Treat no_factory / AUR0004 as intentionally unclaimed for the stock Container model because it guards custom IContainer implementations whose getFactory returns null.

Cite none_resolver_found / AUR0002 as a `DiIssue` product only when the receiver is traced to a stock DefaultResolver.none configuration and the key shape has passed the framework's earlier _jitRegister guards.

Cite null_resolver_from_register / AUR0011 as a `DiIssue` product only when a fresh stock createContainer().get(...) entry uses a source-visible registry key whose register(...) body definitely returns null or undefined.

Cite invalid_new_instance_on_interface / AUR0017 as a `DiIssue` product only when a fresh stock createContainer().get(newInstanceOf/newInstanceForScope(...)) entry wraps a source-visible Aurelia interface key created without a default registration callback.

Leave unable_resolve_key / AUR0008 intentionally unclaimed for the stock Container model unless semantic-runtime admits corrupted/external parent traversal state; Aurelia's ordinary Container.get paths return before that defensive throw.

Cite unable_jit_non_constructor / AUR0009, no_jit_intrinsic_type / AUR0010, or no_jit_interface / AUR0012 as `DiIssue` products only when source-local runtime identity or a future modeled container-state join proves the resolver/factory maps missed.

Do not cite unable_jit_non_constructor / AUR0009 from direct registration object maps or stable syntax-shaped keys; object-map registration is legitimate and stable keys are container-state dependent.
If container API recognition misses a real Aurelia receiver, improve TypeChecker/source-declaration grounding in container-api-recognition.ts rather than falling back to method-name heuristics.

anchors.summary: Runtime-facing DI key request shape used by container lookup branches.
Container lookup failure branch vocabulary for exact framework errors.
Type-backed source recognition for Aurelia container API calls.
Source-backed container API issue materializer for method-local framework failures.
Framework grounding for kernel no* container lookup and resolver failures.

records.summary: DiDependencyCycleIssueMaterializer owns exact kernel cyclic_dependency diagnostics for closed source-visible singleton activation graphs.
records.rationale: Aurelia throws cyclic_dependency when a singleton resolver is re-entered during activation. Semantic-runtime can claim that framework path only when it can form a closed graph from singleton providers, instance-time resolve(...) dependencies, and a concrete container.get(...) entry point.
guidance: Start here before extending cyclic_dependency / AUR0003 coverage beyond interface singleton providers and instance-time resolve(...) dependencies.
Keep this as a source-backed DI graph materializer until controller activation can contribute richer runtime-current-container state.
If the graph needs more provider kinds, ground the provider semantics in framework.di and registration effects before adding app-shaped shortcuts.
